key_insight: SciReasoner is a multimodal foundation model that turns structural data for proteins, small molecules, and crystals into interpretable tokens, improving prediction and reasoning across biology, chemistry, and materials science.
clinical_relevance: Its protein structure reasoning may help support downstream target annotation and hypothesis generation in therapeutic discovery, but it is not a direct clinical decision tool.
method: Discretizes coordinates, topologies, and periodic connectivities into a unified structure-aware vocabulary.
Uses structural tokens as addressable evidence units to generate reasoning traces alongside predictions.
Evaluated across protein, chemistry, and materials benchmarks with homology-controlled tests and expert review.
evidence_signal: Across 86 benchmarks, the model achieved state-of-the-art performance on 67 tasks, and experts preferred or rated its reasoning as comparable in 98% of cases.
watch_item: Performance gains and interpretable traces do not guarantee correct causal explanations, and real-world adoption will require robust validation on unseen structures and workflows.
paper_title: Accurate, Interdisciplinary and Transparent Structure-property Understanding with Deep Native Structural Reasoning
